notes: Dante's judge-loop. HEAR / RULE / STAMP / WITNESS as starters. Buyable pool is FILINGS — pre-existing precedents and signed papers. Authority is the spendable currency. Ledger is the positive doom-clock.

cards.title: HEAR
cards.flavor: Listen to the petitioner state the matter. The brass clock pauses, in the way the brass clock pauses when somebody is being heard. Authority gathers in the room around the desk.

cards.title: RULE
cards.flavor: Write the ruling on the cream stock. Two paragraphs in the language the river-code uses. Costs authority — every ruling spends some of the reservoir.

cards.title: STAMP
cards.flavor: Set the bronze stamp. The date-wheel clicks into today. The stamp leaves a perfect circle, the way it has for forty years. The ledger ticks. The office remembers.

cards.title: WITNESS
cards.flavor: Sign in the witness line. The clerk countersigns. The visitor sees their paper enter the office's permanent record. Their sit-with happens because you saw the paper land in front of you.

cards.title: PRECEDENT
cards.flavor: Pull a volume of the river-code from the shelf. Volumes 9 and 10 are still missing but the others are loyal. The ruling is faster when you can cite.

cards.title: CONSULT CLERK
cards.flavor: Ask the clerk. She clears her throat once on the way to the third shelf. She has been clearing her throat for nineteen years and you have not yet failed to look up.

cards.title: THE DECREE
cards.flavor: A ruling the office itself remembers. The brass clock holds. The visitors here yield their patience one turn longer. Dante's father issued one of these in '74 and people still cite it.

cards.title: APPEAL FORM
cards.flavor: Pull the appellate form from the cabinet. The heavy stock, the one with the watermark. For when you're the second office to hear the case and the first office got it wrong.
